api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/_.purpose: Low-level serialization/deserialization utilities for the generated Python SDK. Provides SdkJSONEncoder, the _Model MutableMapping-based base class (model_base.py), format-aware type converters for datetime/bytes/timedelta/decimal, and the legacy Autorest-style Model/Serializer/Deserializer (serialization.py). These files are fully generated and must not be hand-edited.

patterns.name: Format-dispatch deserialization via get_deserializer()
patterns.description: Always route datetime/bytes/timedelta/decimal deserialization through get_deserializer(annotation, rf). It selects from _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING (keyed by Python type) or _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING_WITHFORMAT (keyed by wire format string like 'rfc7231', 'base64url'). Never hand-roll datetime or bytes parsing.
patterns.example: deserializer = get_deserializer(datetime, rf)  # rf._format may be 'rfc7231' or None; value = deserializer(raw_value)

patterns.name: SdkJSONEncoder for all request body serialization
patterns.description: Pass exclude_readonly=True when building request bodies so read-only fields (visibility=['read']) are stripped. SdkJSONEncoder handles datetime, bytes, timedelta, decimal, and _Model instances via its default() method.
patterns.example: json.dumps(payload, cls=SdkJSONEncoder, exclude_readonly=True)

patterns.name: Two distinct Model base classes — do not mix
patterns.description: model_base.py defines _Model (MutableMapping-backed, uses rest_field annotations). serialization.py defines Model (attribute_map class var, Autorest-style Serializer/Deserializer). _operations.py uses the legacy serialization.Model path via _SERIALIZER; generated dataclass-style models extend _Model.
patterns.example:
# New models: class MyModel(_Model): field: str = rest_field(...)
# Never mix: do not subclass _Model and also declare _attribute_map

patterns.name: _MyMutableMapping cache invalidation on __setitem__
patterns.description: The _MyMutableMapping backing _Model clears _deserialized_<key> attribute cache on every __setitem__. Mutating _data directly bypasses this invalidation. Always go through attribute access or the dict interface (obj['key'] = value or obj.key = value).
patterns.example:
# Correct: obj.name = 'new'  or  obj['name'] = 'new'
# Wrong: obj._data['name'] = 'new'  # bypasses cache invalidation

key_file_guides.role: Defines _Model (MutableMapping-based base for all generated models), rest_field, rest_discriminator, SdkJSONEncoder, and all _deserialize_* helper functions. Core of the new-codegen path.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mutating _data directly on a _Model bypasses the _deserialized_<key> cache-invalidation in __setitem__; always go through attribute or dict interface.

key_file_guides.role: Legacy Autorest-style Model base class, Serializer, Deserializer, RawDeserializer, and key transformers (attribute_transformer, full_restapi_key_transformer). Used by _operations.py via module-level _SERIALIZER singleton.
key_file_guides.watch_for: _SERIALIZER has client_side_validation=False; do not create per-call Serializer instances or re-enable validation.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports SdkJSONEncoder, Model, rest_field, rest_discriminator as the public surface of this utils package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new public symbol added here must also appear in __all__.

anti_patterns: Hand-rolling datetime or bytes serialization instead of using _serialize_datetime / _serialize_bytes / get_deserializer()
Editing model_base.py or serialization.py directly — they are generated and overwritten by make gen-api
Creating per-request Serializer() instances — _SERIALIZER is a module-level singleton with client_side_validation=False
Importing from corehttp internals not already imported in these files (adds fragile transitive dependency)
Subclassing _Model and also declaring _attribute_map — these are mutually exclusive codegen paths

decisions.decision: Two separate Model base classes coexist: _Model (MutableMapping, rest_field) and serialization.Model (attribute_map, Autorest)
decisions.rationale: SDK was generated with a newer codegen (_Model) but retains the legacy Autorest Model for backwards compatibility with older client patterns and XML support via serialization.py.

decisions.decision: _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING_WITHFORMAT separates format-aware converters from type-based converters
decisions.rationale: The same Python type (datetime) maps to multiple wire formats (rfc3339, rfc7231, unix-timestamp). Splitting into two maps lets get_deserializer() check format first, then fall back to type-only dispatch.

code_examples.scenario: Deserialize a datetime field with format awareness from a rest_field annotation
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated._utils.model_base import get_deserializer
# rf is the _RestField instance whose _format may be 'rfc7231', 'unix-timestamp', or None
deserializer = get_deserializer(datetime, rf)
value = deserializer(raw_value)  # returns a timezone-aware datetime

api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/m.purpose: Auto-generated Python model classes and enums for the entire OpenMeter API surface. Every request/response body type (~200+ classes in _models.py) and every string enum (_enums.py) is defined here; this is the single source of truth for Python-typed API objects in the SDK. Do not edit _models.py or _enums.py — only _patch.py is safe to modify.

patterns.name: rest_field attribute declaration with visibility and name mapping
patterns.description: Each field is declared as a class variable using rest_field(name='camelCaseName', visibility=[...], format='rfc3339') to map Python snake_case to JSON camelCase. Required fields have no default; optional fields are Optional[T] with = rest_field(...). The visibility list controls which CRUD operations include the field.
patterns.example: created_at: datetime.datetime = rest_field(name='createdAt', visibility=['read'], format='rfc3339')

patterns.name: Dual @overload __init__ pattern
patterns.description: Every model class has two @overload signatures: one with keyword args (for IDE completion of required fields), one with mapping: Mapping[str, Any] (for raw-dict construction from parsed JSON). The real __init__ always calls super().__init__(*args, **kwargs).

patterns.name: CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta for all string enums
patterns.description: All enums in _enums.py inherit from (str, Enum) with metaclass=CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta from corehttp.utils. This enables case-insensitive lookup by string value so wire comparisons work regardless of server casing.

patterns.name: _patch.py as the only safe customization point
patterns.description: _patch.py is preserved across regenerations. Add helper methods, monkey-patches, or additional public symbols here. patch_sdk() must remain callable. New symbols must also be listed in _patch.__all__ to appear in models.__all__.
patterns.example:
# _patch.py
__all__ = ['my_helper']
def my_helper(): ...
def patch_sdk(): pass  # must remain callable

key_file_guides.role: Contains all ~200+ generated dataclass-style model classes extending _Model from _utils/model_base. The authoritative definition of every API request/response shape.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by make gen-api. All customizations must go in _patch.py.

key_file_guides.role: All string enums used across models and operations. Every enum uses CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta for wire-value matching.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. Never add plain str Enum without CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all models and enums, applies _patch.py via _patch_sdk(). The __all__ list is the authoritative public API of this subpackage.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. New public symbols added in _patch.py must appear in _patch.__all__ to propagate here.

key_file_guides.role: The only file safe to hand-edit. Place SDK customizations that must survive regeneration here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain callable even if empty. Never remove it.

anti_patterns: Editing _models.py or _enums.py directly — overwritten by make gen-api
Subclassing generated models outside of _patch.py — breaks regeneration idempotency
Using plain str Enum without CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta — breaks case-insensitive wire value matching
Adding new public symbols to _patch.py without listing them in _patch.__all__ — they won't appear in models.__all__
Constructing models with positional arguments — use keyword args or a Mapping dict; positional args are not supported by the overload signatures

decisions.decision: All models are MutableMapping-backed (_Model) rather than plain dataclasses
decisions.rationale: Enables dict-style access and JSON serialization without a separate to_dict() step, matching the corehttp pipeline's expectation for request bodies and the SdkJSONEncoder's iteration over items().

decisions.decision: _patch.py is preserved across regenerations as the sole customization point
decisions.rationale: Generated code must remain pristine for automated regeneration; _patch.py provides a stable escape hatch that survives make gen-api without merge conflicts.

code_examples.scenario: Construct a model using typed kwargs or a raw dict from parsed JSON
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated.models import Customer, AddonCreate, AppType

# Typed keyword construction (IDE completes required fields)
customer = Customer(name='Acme', external_mapping_key='acme-001')

# Raw dict construction from parsed JSON response
customer = Customer({'name': 'Acme', 'externalMappingKey': 'acme-001'})

# Enum usage — case-insensitive
app_type = AppType('STRIPE')  # == AppType.STRIPE

api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/o.purpose: Auto-generated operation classes that implement every OpenMeter API endpoint as typed Python methods grouped by resource (CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations, BillingProfilesOperations, etc.). Each class uses the corehttp pipeline to build, send, and deserialize HTTP requests. Do not edit _operations.py — only _patch.py is safe to modify.

patterns.name: build_*_request free functions for request construction
patterns.description: Every operation is preceded by a build_<resource>_<verb>_request(...) free function that constructs the HttpRequest (URL path formatting, query params, headers) using the module-level _SERIALIZER singleton. The operation method calls this builder then sends via self._client.send_request. Never build HttpRequest objects manually outside this pattern.

patterns.name: ClsType optional callback for result interception
patterns.description: Every operation method accepts an optional cls: ClsType kwarg — a callback(pipeline_response, deserialized, headers) that can intercept or transform the result. Pass None (the default) for standard use. Never remove this parameter from generated signatures.
patterns.example: ClsType = Optional[Callable[[PipelineResponse[HttpRequest, HttpResponse], T, dict[str, Any]], Any]]

patterns.name: map_error for typed HTTP error mapping before deserialization
patterns.description: Operations call map_error(status_code=response.status_code, response=response, error_map={404: ResourceNotFoundError, 409: ResourceExistsError, ...}) before deserializing success responses. Never catch HttpResponseError without calling map_error first.
patterns.example:
error_map = {404: ResourceNotFoundError, 409: ResourceExistsError}
map_error(status_code=response.status_code, response=response, error_map=error_map)

patterns.name: Module-level _SERIALIZER singleton — do not reinstantiate
patterns.description: _SERIALIZER = Serializer() is a module-level singleton with client_side_validation=False. Never create per-call Serializer() instances. All query/header/URL serialization goes through _SERIALIZER.query(), _SERIALIZER.header(), _SERIALIZER.url().
patterns.example:
_SERIALIZER = Serializer()
_SERIALIZER.client_side_validation = False
# Usage: _SERIALIZER.query('page', page, 'int')

patterns.name: ItemPaged for paginated list operations
patterns.description: List operations that return paginated collections return ItemPaged[T] from corehttp.paging. Callers iterate the result directly; the pager handles next-page fetching transparently.

key_file_guides.role: All ~30 generated operation classes. Each is initialized with a PipelineClient and OpenMeterClientConfiguration. Contains all build_*_request helpers and the send+deserialize logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by make gen-api. _SERIALIZER is module-level; do not create per-request instances.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all Operations classes and applies _patch.py via _patch_sdk(). The __all__ list is authoritative.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. Any symbols added in _patch.py must appear in _patch.__all__ to be included in __all__.

key_file_guides.role: The only file safe to hand-edit. Override or extend generated operation class methods here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain callable. Methods added here must not conflict with generated signatures.

anti_patterns: Editing _operations.py directly — overwritten by make gen-api
Building HttpRequest objects manually outside the build_*_request free-function pattern — breaks serialization consistency
Catching HttpResponseError without calling map_error first — loses typed exception mapping (ResourceNotFoundError, ResourceExistsError, etc.)
Creating per-request Serializer() instances instead of using the module-level _SERIALIZER singleton
Removing the cls: ClsType parameter from any operation method signature

decisions.decision: Request construction is split into build_*_request free functions separate from the send+deserialize logic in Operations class methods
decisions.rationale: Enables unit-testing request construction (URL, headers, params) without an HTTP client, and keeps the send path uniform across all operations.

decisions.decision: Operations classes are grouped by resource (CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations, etc.) rather than by HTTP method
decisions.rationale: Resource-grouped classes match the TypeSpec interface groupings and make it easy to find all operations for a given domain without scanning the entire file.

code_examples.scenario: Invoke a paginated list operation and a single-resource get through the operations classes
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated.operations import CustomersOperations, InvoicesOperations

# ops is injected by the top-level OpenMeterClient; not constructed directly
# Paginated list — iterate ItemPaged[Customer]
for customer in ops.customers.list(page=1, page_size=20):
    print(customer.name)

# Single resource get
invoice = ops.invoices.get(invoice_id='01HXYZ...')

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/billing.purpose: TypeSpec schema-only package defining the v1 billing API surface — billing profiles, customer overrides, and invoice lifecycle endpoints. All .tsp files compile into api/openapi.yaml via `make gen-api`; no runtime code lives here.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model, union, enum, and interface
patterns.description: Every public declaration carries @friendlyName to produce stable, human-readable Go type names. Missing @friendlyName causes autogenerated names that break SDK contracts on schema change.
patterns.example:
@friendlyName("BillingProfile")
model BillingProfile { ... }

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on every field
patterns.description: Fields must be annotated with Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update as appropriate. Fields without @visibility default to all lifecycles and expose internal state in create/update payloads.
patterns.example:
@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)
supplier: BillingParty;

patterns.name: Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic types
patterns.description: All union variants use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) so the discriminator field is inlined. Adding a new union arm requires both the arm in the union AND a model with a matching `type` literal field.

patterns.name: Spread (...) and OmitProperties<T> for model composition, not extends
patterns.description: Models reuse shared fields via spread (e.g. ...global.Resource, ...OmitProperties<T, ...>). Using extends where spread is expected changes property semantics in generated SDK types.

patterns.name: Explicit @operationId on every interface operation
patterns.description: Every operation must carry @operationId to produce stable Go function names in the generated server stub. Omitting it causes autogenerated names that may change across TypeSpec compiler versions.

patterns.name: namespace OpenMeter on all declarations
patterns.description: Every model, union, enum, and interface must be declared inside namespace OpenMeter. Missing namespace causes the symbol to land in the global scope and fail to compile as part of the package.

patterns.name: main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files
patterns.description: Every new .tsp file added to this folder must be imported in main.tsp. A file not imported there compiles in isolation but produces no output in api/openapi.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: imports all sibling and child .tsp files and declares namespace usage directives.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new .tsp file without a matching import here — the file silently produces no output.

key_file_guides.role: Defines BillingProfile, BillingProfileCreate, BillingProfileReplaceUpdate, BillingWorkflow and its sub-settings (collection, invoicing, payment, tax), app references, and CRUD interface for /api/v1/billing/profiles.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> — adding a field to BillingProfile without controlling its visibility on the replace model will leak read-only fields into the update payload.

key_file_guides.role: Defines customer override CRUD at /api/v1/billing/customers/{customerId} and BillingProfileCustomerOverrideWithDetails response merging override + base profile.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BillingProfileCustomerWorkflowOverride has app fields annotated @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — they must never appear in create/update inputs.

key_file_guides.role: Defines invoice listing/get/delete/update/action endpoints across three interface groups (InvoicesEndpoints, InvoiceEndpoints, CustomerInvoiceEndpoints) and request/response models for invoice actions (void, approve, advance, retry, snapshot-quantities).
key_file_guides.watch_for: VoidInvoiceLineAction is a discriminated union with envelope:none — adding a new void action type requires both a new union arm AND a new model with a matching `type` literal field.

key_file_guides.role: Child package entry: imports all invoice sub-models (invoice.tsp, discounts.tsp, party.tsp, pay.tsp, tax.tsp, docref.tsp). Invoice entity field shape lives in the child package, not the parent invoices.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not define invoice entity fields in the parent invoices.tsp — that file only defines endpoints and action input/output models.

anti_patterns: Adding runtime or business logic — this folder is schema-only; all logic belongs in openmeter/billing/
Editing generated files (api/openapi.yaml, api/api.gen.go) instead of the .tsp source
Omitting @friendlyName on new models, unions, or enums — causes unstable generated Go type names that break SDK contracts
Adding fields without @visibility decorators — fields default to all lifecycles and expose internal state on create/update requests
Using extends for model composition instead of spread (...) or OmitProperties — changes property inheritance semantics in generated output

decisions.decision: Visibility-controlled fields instead of separate request/response types
decisions.rationale: Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Read/Create/Update) on a single model reduces duplication and ensures the OpenAPI generator derives correct request/response schemas automatically without maintaining parallel type hierarchies.

decisions.decision: Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic workflow alignment and void-action types
decisions.rationale: envelope:none inlines the discriminator field directly in the object, matching the Go domain types in openmeter/billing/ which use a Type string field rather than a wrapper object.

decisions.decision: BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> with an explicit workflow override field
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceReplaceModel auto-derives update payload from read model visibility; the explicit workflow field override allows different update semantics for workflow sub-config without duplicating the whole BillingProfile tree.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing workflow action endpoint with a discriminated input type

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/entitlement.purpose: TypeSpec schema-only package defining the v1 entitlements API surface — subjects-scoped entitlement CRUD, grant management, customer access checks, and shared value/history/reset sub-routes. All V1 symbols are deprecated in favour of v2 equivalents in the v2/ sub-folder; new entitlement work belongs there.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none for polymorphic entitlement types
patterns.description: The Entitlement union and EntitlementCreateInputs union use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }). Adding a new entitlement type requires a new arm in BOTH unions plus a new model with a matching `type` literal field.

patterns.name: OmitProperties<T, "field1" | "field2"> for model composition
patterns.description: Response models spread OmitProperties over a base create-input type and add read-only fields, avoiding full redefinition and keeping create/read models in sync automatically when the base type changes.

patterns.name: #deprecated + #suppress pair on every V1 symbol with a V2 successor
patterns.description: Every V1 model, union, and operation that has a V2 successor must carry both #deprecated and #suppress. Missing either causes compiler warnings that break `make gen-api`.

patterns.name: Explicit @operationId on every operation
patterns.description: All operations carry @operationId to ensure stable generated Go function names. Omitting it causes name instability across TypeSpec compiler upgrades.

patterns.name: main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files
patterns.description: A new .tsp file in this folder must be imported in main.tsp (which also cross-imports productcatalog/features.tsp) or it produces no output in api/openapi.yaml.

patterns.name: Separate interface groups per distinct URL prefix
patterns.description: Each interface maps to exactly one URL prefix so adding operations does not pollute another prefix's operation set.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: imports all sibling .tsp files including the cross-package productcatalog/features.tsp. No model or interface declarations live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting to import a new .tsp file — it compiles in isolation but is invisible to the full package output.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Entitlement discriminated union, EntitlementCreateInputs union, EntitlementSharedFields, all three per-type create-input models (metered/boolean/static), and the admin list/get endpoints at /api/v1/entitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new entitlement type variant requires updating BOTH the Entitlement read union AND the EntitlementCreateInputs create union atomically, or the discriminator will be incomplete.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all V1 subject-scoped entitlement CRUD and grant endpoints at /api/v1/subjects/{subjectIdOrKey}/entitlements. All operations are deprecated and point to V2 equivalents.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add new operations here — they belong in the v2 sub-folder. Every operation here carries #deprecated + #suppress.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Grant, GrantCreateInput, ExpirationPeriod, ExpirationDuration, and the global /api/v1/grants admin list/void endpoints. Already imports @typespec/http and uses TypeSpec.Http.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing `import "@typespec/http"` and `using TypeSpec.Http` in new files referencing these models will cause compilation failure.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CustomerAccess and the customer-scoped access-check endpoints (getCustomerAccess, getCustomerEntitlementValue) at /api/v1/customers/{customerIdOrKey}.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerAccess.entitlements is typed as Record<EntitlementValue> — do not split into a separate typed map without updating all SDK consumers.

anti_patterns: Adding new V1 operations or models in this folder instead of the v2 sub-folder — all V1 entitlement APIs are deprecated
Adding a new entitlement type variant to only one of the two discriminated unions (Entitlement or EntitlementCreateInputs) — both must be updated atomically
Omitting #deprecated + #suppress pair on new V1 symbols — causes compiler warnings that break `make gen-api`
Defining models without @friendlyName — autogenerated names can clash with or shadow V2 names in the same namespace
Adding HTTP decorators to a new .tsp file without `import "@typespec/http"` and `using TypeSpec.Http` — causes compilation failure

decisions.decision: V1 symbols are kept but fully deprecated in favour of V2 equivalents in the v2 sub-folder
decisions.rationale: Breaking API changes require a deprecation window; keeping V1 symbols with #deprecated annotations lets the compiler warn SDK consumers while the generator still emits the endpoints for backward-compatible clients.

decisions.decision: OmitProperties composition instead of standalone read-model redefinitions
decisions.rationale: Avoids maintaining two parallel type hierarchies for create vs. read; when a create-input field changes, the read model reflects it automatically unless explicitly omitted.

decisions.decision: EntitlementValue shared across all entitlement types with optional type-specific fields
decisions.rationale: A single EntitlementValue model with optional metered-specific fields (balance, usage, overage) and shared hasAccess allows the /value endpoint to return a uniform shape regardless of entitlement type, reducing client branching logic.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new V2-only entitlement endpoint (this work belongs in v2/, not here)
